reasoning: Friedrich Merz became Chancellor of Germany on May 6, 2025. He formed a 'grand coalition' with the SPD. While there were calls for his resignation on the very day he was sworn in (due to failing the first parliamentary vote) and mentions of typical coalition discord, recent news from November 2025 shows him still actively serving as Chancellor, making policy statements (e.g. November 6, 2025 statements on European patriotism for the steel industry, and November 4 statements on Syrian repatriation). With only less than two months remaining in 2025 (November 9 to December 31) and no significant evidence of an impending government collapse or resignation, the probability of him ceasing to be Chancellor before the end of the year is very low. Grand coalitions in Germany tend to be stable, and German Chancellors rarely resign within their first year in office. Therefore, a low probability assignment is justified.
